ccel.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

title: Concerning the devil and demons.

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: He who from among these angelic powers was set over the earthly realm, and into whose hands God committed the guardianship of the earth, was not made wicked in nature but was good, and made for good ends, and received from his Creator no trace whatever of evil in himself.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But he did not sustain the brightness and the honour which the Creator had bestowed on him, and of his free choice was changed from what was in harmony to what was at variance with his nature, and became roused against God Who created him, and determined to rise in rebellion against Him: and he was the first to depart from good and become evil.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For evil is nothing else than absence of goodness, just as darkness also is absence of light.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For goodness is the light of the mind, and, similarly, evil is the darkness of the mind.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Light, therefore, being the work of the Creator and being made good (for God saw all that He made, and behold they were exceeding good) produced darkness at His free-will.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But along with him an innumerable host of angels subject to him were torn away and followed him and shared in his fall.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Wherefore, being of the same nature as the angels, they became wicked, turning away at their own free choice from good to evil.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Hence they have no power or strength against any one except what God in His dispensation hath conceded to them, as for instance, against Job and those swine that are mentioned in the Gospels.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But when God has made the concession they do prevail, and are changed and transformed into any form whatever in which they wish to appear.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Of the future both the angels of God and the demons are alike ignorant: yet they make predictions.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: God reveals the future to the angels and commands them to prophesy, and so what they say comes to pass.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But the demons also make predictions, sometimes because they see what is happening at a distance, and sometimes merely making guesses: hence much that they say is false and they should not be believed, even although they do often, in the way we have said, tell what is true.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: All wickedness, then, and all impure passions are the work of their mind.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But while the liberty to attack man has been granted to them, they have not the strength to over-master any one: for we have it in our power to receive or not to receive the attack.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Wherefore there has been prepared for the devil and his demons, and those who follow him, fire unquenchable and everlasting punishment.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Note, further, that what in the case of man is death is a fall in the case of angels.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For after the fall there is no possibility of repentance for them, just as after death there is for men no repentance.
